risks: All services have publicNetworkAccess enabled — no private endpoints configured, increasing attack surface
Storage account uses Standard_LRS with no zone or geo redundancy — single-zone failure could cause data loss for sales data
No Azure Key Vault resource for secure credential and secret management across Data Factory, Databricks, and Synapse
No monitoring or logging resources (Log Analytics workspace, diagnostic settings) for operational visibility
Databricks workspace lacks VNet injection — WAF strongly recommends virtual network injection for network isolation and egress control
Synapse managed virtual network is set to 'default' but data exfiltration protection is not explicitly enabled
Databricks and Synapse managed identities are not explicitly configured — only Data Factory has SystemAssigned identity declared
correctionsRecommended: Add an Azure Key Vault resource for centralized credential and secret management, and integrate with Data Factory and Databricks
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on all resources for monitoring, alerting, and troubleshooting
Upgrade storage redundancy from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS or Standard_GRS for resilience against zone or regional failures
Configure private endpoints for Storage, Data Factory, Databricks, and Synapse to eliminate public network exposure
Enable VNet injection for the Databricks workspace with custom NSG rules and secure cluster connectivity
Enable data exfiltration protection on the Synapse workspace by setting managedVirtualNetwork outbound restrictions to approved targets only
Add SystemAssigned managed identity to Databricks and Synapse workspace resources for consistent identity-based authentication
Add resource tags (environment, project, costCenter) across all resources for governance and cost tracking
Consider adding Data Factory dependency on Databricks to reflect the orchestration relationship in the ETL pipeline
